_comment: Big Muddy Radio station ID stingers — ElevenLabs generation manifest. Edit this file to add/remove/change stingers. Run `node scripts/stingers/generate.mjs` to generate MP3s, then `bash scripts/stingers/deploy.sh` to ship them to the Mac mini. Voice IDs are from Chase's ElevenLabs account library (listed at https://elevenlabs.io/app/voice-library).

stingers.script: Y'all listening to the Big Muddy, riding the river from Memphis to New Orleans. The soul of the South. Amen.
Ralph Barker here, live from Natchez, Mississippi. You're tuned to Big Muddy Radio. Twenty-four hours a day. The soul of the South.
In a town by the river, the music never stops. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South, after dark.
It's late. You're driving. You're alone. And we're here. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South.
Now honey, pour yourself somethin' cold and stay a while. You're on Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South.
Big Muddy Radio! Memphis to New Orleans! The only station playing the real soul of the South. Every hour. Every day.
Music was born here. On this river. Big Muddy Radio. Broadcasting the soul of the South, from Natchez, Mississippi.
Don't tell a soul. It's just us. Big Muddy Radio. Whispering the soul of the South, at midnight.
Brothers and sisters, the river carries the music. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South, been here longer than you have.
The river don't forget a name. Neither do we. Big Muddy Radio, broadcasting to every crossroads from Memphis to the Gulf.
Turn it up, child. This is the church of the Big Muddy. The soul of the South on every frequency.
If the music's good, you don't need a sermon. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South. Amen and goodnight.
This is Ralph Barker. You're listening to Big Muddy Radio, broadcasting live from Natchez, Mississippi. Don't touch that dial.
From the bluff above the Mississippi, this is Big Muddy Radio. Ralph Barker, your host, keeping the lights on all night long.
Ralph Barker at the controls. Big Muddy Radio, the soul of the South, streaming from the Blues Room in Natchez.
You made it this far. That means something. Ralph Barker, Big Muddy Radio, the soul of the South. Stay with us.
Rain on the windshield. Static on the dial. Then a voice cut through. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South.
She said she'd meet me at the river. She didn't say which one. I've been here ever since. Big Muddy Radio.
Midnight in Natchez. The music knows things you don't. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South.
Every river hides a body. Every bar hides a song. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South, after dark.
Can't sleep. Can't stop. That's the Big Muddy way. Big Muddy Radio, the soul of the South, all night long.
Reed Latenight. Big Muddy Radio. This is the hour when the river talks back.
Slow it down. Feel the river. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South, the way you remember it.
Midnight on the Mississippi. Nothing else matters for the next three minutes. Big Muddy Radio.
Come sit down, baby. I made iced tea. The radio's already on. Big Muddy Radio, the soul of the South.
You remember when they used to play this on the porch radio? We still do. Big Muddy Radio.
The porch light's on. The door's unlocked. The music's playing. Big Muddy Radio, y'all. The soul of the South.
I don't know the song title. I know how it feels. That's enough. Big Muddy Radio, the soul of the South.
Big Muddy Radio! Broadcasting from the Blues Room in historic Natchez, Mississippi! The only station that plays it the way it was meant to be played!
Twenty-four hours a day! Seven days a week! Big Muddy Radio! The soul of the South, non-stop, coast to coast!
From Memphis to New Orleans! The corridor that built American music! Big Muddy Radio! The soul of the South!
You're tuned to Big Muddy Radio! The only station recording live from Natchez, Mississippi! Turn it up!
The music is a prayer and the river is the church. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South.
They sang it first in the fields. Then in the churches. Then in the juke joints. Now on Big Muddy Radio.
Every song has a name. Every name has a story. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South, every hour.
This music was here before you. It will be here after you. Big Muddy Radio, from Natchez, Mississippi.
Closer. Closer than that. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South. Only us, only now.
The river's asleep. The town's asleep. But not you. Not me. Big Muddy Radio.
Keep it between us. The good songs, the late hours, the river. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South.
Stay with me a little longer. The music isn't done. Big Muddy Radio. Midnight on the Mississippi.
Praise the good Lord and pass the guitar. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South, amen amen.
This is Big Muddy Radio. Ralph Barker saying, the next song is worth waiting for. Stay tuned.
The bar was closing. The music kept going. Somewhere out there, someone was still listening. Big Muddy Radio.
No commercials, no interruptions, no hurry. Big Muddy Radio. The soul of the South, after hours.
Now you mind your manners and enjoy the music. Big Muddy Radio, sweetheart. The soul of the South.
Big Muddy Radio! Live from Natchez, Mississippi! Music, stories, and the sound of the river! All day, every day!
Let the music carry you. The river has. It still does. Big Muddy Radio, the soul of the South.
One more song. One more song. One more song. Big Muddy Radio. We don't sleep either.
